nodes.name: 山地森林
nodes.summary: 以成熟阔叶林和混交林为主的山地森林系统。

nodes.name: 森林砍伐
nodes.summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

nodes.name: 阿魯群島
nodes.summary: 阿魯群島是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联33种鸟类，包括Black-billed brushturkey、Ivory-billed coucal、戈氏姬地鸠等33种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 新幾內亞島
nodes.summary: 新幾內亞島是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联312种鸟类，包括南方鶴鴕、鵲鵝、细斑树鸭等312种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 拉賈安帕特群島
nodes.summary: 拉賈安帕特群島是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联4种鸟类，包括Dwarf fruit dove、Spice imperial pigeon、長尾鵟等4种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 亞彭島
nodes.summary: 亞彭島是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联6种鸟类，包括長尾鵟、Blue-black kingfisher、Dusky lory等6种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 斯考滕群島
nodes.summary: 斯考滕群島是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联2种鸟类，包括Geelvink fruit dove、長尾鵟，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 長尾鵟
nodes.summary: 長尾鵟（Long-tailed honey buzzard，学名 Henicopernis longicauda），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联5个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括山地森林，主要威胁包括森林砍伐。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is found in New Guinea and some neighboring island groups.Raja Ampat, Aru, Biak, and Yapen groups

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: The Long-tailed honey buzzard is native to New Guinea and its surrounding islands. These include the Bay Islands of Japen and Biak, the Northwest Islands of Misool, Batanta, Salawati and Waigeo, the Aru Islands, the Southeast Islands of Fergusson and Normanby. On mainland New Guinea, they are reported to be found in the Trans-Fly savannah ecoregion on the southern end of the island, but also more broadly throughout New Guinea, except for the mountainous regions of the Western, Central and Eastern ranges. The Long-tailed honey buzzard is found typically in the regions’ lowland rainforests, hill forests and montane forests. It is a rainforest specialist and is restricted to New Guinea's rainforest habitats.{{Cite journal |last=Gamauf |first=A

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The most pressing threat to the Long-tailed honey buzzard is deforestation. Although considered of least concern status, population numbers, trends and biology are poorly researched, and populations in some regions may be declining significantly
